result:
Clean build. 

**Root cause.** `os_time` at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/os_lib.rs:556` called `state.set_top(1)`, which resolves to the inherent `LuaState::set_top` taking an **absolute** `StackIdx`. The public Lua C-API `lua_settop(L, 1)` is **relative to the current frame's `func`**, so what should have kept the function's arg at index 1 instead truncated the stack to absolute slot 1 (which was below the call frame), wiping the table argument. Subsequent `get_field(-1, "year")` then saw nil — hence `attempt to index a nil value`, raised from `lua_vm::vm::finish_get` (vm.rs:595).

**Fix.** Replaced `state.set_top(1)` with `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, 1)?` (the frame-relative form), matching the same fix already applied in `loadlib.rs:977` with an identical port-note. Removed the temporary `eprintln`/backtrace instrumentation in `lua-types/src/error.rs`.

**State after fix.** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Did not re-run the file program per the prompt — the mega-loop will re-test. The bug fixes only the `os.time(table)` indexing failure on line 797; the `load(os.date(...))()` chain on line 798–800 will still hit failures because strftime specifiers are still stubbed (a separate Phase-B item, not this bug).
